verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted cysteine-rich protein expressed in epididymis and bound to sperm. It is not a canonical secreted-only protein — it has documented stable association with sperm cell surfaces as part of the fertilization recognition complex. The protein is involved in sperm-egg plasma membrane interactions and binds to specific sperm surface domains. This constitutes stable non-covalent association with a cellular surface during the reproductive process, restricting display to germline cells. The tissue and developmental restriction (epididymal/gamete-specific) and the stable anchoring to sperm surface warrant contextual classification rather than secreted_only.
